status: local-only draft; no Microsoft 365 tenant write occurred

values.Title: B5 M365 Interaction Agent one-writer posture selected
values.Decision: Select one canonical Guided AI Labs agent lane named M365 Interaction Agent. Prime Boiler 365 setup remains a separate account/profile/session lane. The current posture remains supervised delegated and local-first. No new app registration, admin consent, SharePoint Selected grant, Exchange Application RBAC grant, broad Graph/Teams/Planner grant, or unattended production bridge adapter is approved. Immediate agent write surface after approval is limited to one Agent Action Log row with ActionStatus=Suggested per selected CRM signal through scripts/Invoke-M365NewSignalTriage.ps1 -Apply. Guided AI Labs and Guided AI Journey intake flows, plus the New Signal Teams alert flow, remain accepted create-only/source-ingress automations and are not agent decision writers.
values.Rationale: B1-B4 proved a useful CRM New Signal -> Teams alert -> triage -> Suggested-row loop without updating CRM, sending external messages, creating tasks, changing permissions, or granting new app power. G0-only is too narrow because the Suggested review queue is useful; app or G2/G3 authority is too broad before more source proofs. This posture preserves Adam approval while allowing B6 Guided AI Journey intake to use the same CRM signal lane.

blockedActions: CRM updates, merges, suppressions, lookup conversions, dedupe actions, or status changes
external email, Teams chat, SMS, call, voicemail, QUO action, or client commitment
public website-to-CRM POST route or tenant secret in a website repo
app registration, consent grant, SharePoint Selected permission, Exchange Application RBAC, Graph/Teams/Planner scope grant, guest invite, sharing change, tenant policy change, or delete
sourceIngressExceptions: GAIL - Guided AI Labs intake to CRM (create-only)
GAIL - Guided AI Journey intake to CRM (create-only)
nextSourceProof: B6 Guided AI Journey client intake through the existing Form or website CTA/embed into CRM - New Signals
